2026.06.18.90.title: Circuit-level view
highlights: Officials assigned to a circuit now see activity and insights for their own circuit, rather than the whole district

2026.06.18.63.title: Updated school lists for 8 provinces
highlights: The school picker now uses the latest DBE masterlists for Eastern Cape, Free State, Gauteng, KwaZulu-Natal, Limpopo, Mpumalanga, North West, and Northern Cape
Every school in these provinces now carries its official EMIS number, so visit reports match departmental records exactly

2026.06.18.61.title: A much faster app
highlights: The app is now 60% smaller and opens noticeably faster, especially on slow connections — the school list is loaded quietly in the background after you sign in

2026.06.18.60.title: Open each message on its own
highlights: Tap any message in your Inbox to open it on its own screen, with the full text and a clear way back
Messages you can act on — like being tagged by a colleague — have a reply box; programme updates are read-only, so there is nothing to answer

2026.06.18.59.title: A smoother Inbox
highlights: Opening the Inbox no longer redraws the whole screen — it appears instantly, with no flicker

2026.06.18.58.title: Inbox now opens properly
highlights: Fixed the error that stopped the Inbox from loading your messages
Added clear Close buttons at the top and bottom of the Inbox

2026.06.18.57.title: Inbox opens reliably, neatly placed
highlights: Fixed the Inbox button not responding on some phones — a stray second tap was closing it the instant it opened
The Inbox button now sits neatly above Sign out in the header

2026.06.18.56.title: A clearly labelled Inbox button
highlights: The bell at the top is now a clearly labelled Inbox button, so you always know where your messages live

2026.06.18.55.title: Inbox, refined
highlights: The bell now opens a single, calm Inbox — messages and activity in one list, newest first, no jumpy double-loading
Tap a message to expand it and reply right there; nothing whisks you away to another screen

2026.06.18.54.title: Your new in-app inbox
highlights: The bell at the top now opens your inbox: when a colleague tags you on a support need or replies to you, the message stays here — no more hunting through email
You can reply to tags and messages directly inside the app, and your colleague is notified
Mobile layout fixes: overlapping labels on small screens (including the Intervention Activity Tracker tab) are resolved

2026.06.18.53.title: Mobile layout fixes after the bigger text
highlights: Fixed overlapping labels on small screens — including the Intervention Activity Tracker tab — by restoring the phone-specific layout rules that were being silently overridden
Buttons, tabs, and cards across the dashboard and toolkit now wrap and space correctly on phones

2026.06.18.52.title: A fresher home feed with real dates and times
highlights: Each entry on your home feed now shows exactly when it happened — like Today, 09:41 or Wed 9 Jul, 21:42 — in a small badge on the card
Visit cards carry a subtle teal accent and items needing your attention carry orange, so the feed is easier to scan

2026.06.18.51.title: Easier-to-read text throughout
highlights: Text across the whole app is now larger and easier to read — nearly 500 places where writing was small have been increased, while keeping the layout familiar

2026.06.18.50.title: Clear message when your session ends
highlights: If you are signed out after a period of inactivity, the app now tells you plainly and takes you straight back to sign-in with your email already filled in — no more unexplained blank screens

2026.06.18.49.title: Clearer report timing and easy help access
highlights: After you submit, the app now tells you your report arrives within a few minutes and to check spam the first time
A Need help? contact link now appears at the bottom of every screen, including sign-in

2026.06.18.48.title: No draft saved on Step 1
highlights: A draft is now only kept once you move past Step 1, so tapping into the form and leaving no longer creates a saved intervention to resume

2026.06.18.47.title: PIN reset fixed end to end
highlights: Setting a new PIN after a reset now saves correctly and lets you sign straight back in
Every device now refreshes to the latest version automatically on next open

2026.06.18.46.title: Email notifications restored
highlights: Fixed the connection fault that stopped sign-in notifications and PIN reset emails from being sent

2026.06.18.45.title: Clearer reset feedback
highlights: The PIN reset screen now tells you exactly what happened instead of always saying Done

2026.06.18.44.title: Faster PIN reset
highlights: The reset request now responds immediately — your email is on its way while the screen confirms

2026.06.18.43.title: PIN reset, bigger and better
highlights: The PIN reset email now arrives reliably, from the Tracker's own address
Bigger, easier reset and back buttons on the sign-in screens

2026.06.18.42.title: Reset your own PIN
highlights: Forgot your PIN? Tap ‘Reset it by email’ on the sign-in screen — a link arrives to set a new one yourself
No need to wait for the Interventions Manager

2026.06.18.41.title: Record button up top
highlights: The Record a school visit or intervention button now sits at the top of your feed — no scrolling needed

2026.06.18.40.title: Bigger text everywhere
highlights: Larger, clearer text across the whole app — the form, your dashboard, and the toolkit now match the feed
Field labels and options are noticeably easier to read

2026.06.18.39.title: Easier on the eyes
highlights: Bigger, clearer text across the whole home feed — headings, stories, badges and buttons
Slightly larger profile circles and touch-friendly spacing

2026.06.18.38.title: Every visit tells its story
highlights: Timeline items now show what was found, what support was given, and what was committed — tap ‘see more’ for the full story
Quiet recognition badges: location verified, informed by DDD data, colleagues brought in, repeat visits
A weekly pulse tops the timeline with visits and schools reached since Monday
Home is now the first tab on your phone — one tap back to your feed from anywhere
Reopening or refreshing the app takes you straight to your feed

2026.06.18.37.title: The full two-month timeline
highlights: Below your daily items, the feed now carries every visit logged across the programme in the past two months
Your own visits appear as You; older items show their actual date
Tap Show earlier activity to go further back

2026.06.18.36.title: A feed worth checking daily
highlights: New data announcements: when term results land on the DDD Dashboard, your feed says so
Two fresh data stories from your area every day — a school needing attention and a bright spot worth learning from
Programme pulse: interventions and schools reached this year
Peer updates now only appear when they are recent

2026.06.18.35.title: A true timeline
highlights: Your home feed is now a flowing timeline — who did what, where, and when across the programme
Recent visits by fellow officials now appear as individual updates
Cleaner, lighter look throughout the feed

2026.06.18.34.title: Home button and feed refresh
highlights: A Home tab now takes you back to your feed from anywhere
Tap Refresh on the feed to pull the latest activity — it also refreshes itself when you return to the app
If you have a saved draft, it now appears at the top of your feed

2026.06.18.33.title: A feed that changes every day
highlights: A daily data spotlight from your own district — different every day, linking to the DDD Dashboard
The feed now tells you when a colleague tags you, and confirms when your report has been emailed
Peer activity now shows today's movement, not just the week

2026.06.18.32.title: A richer home feed
highlights: Your home feed now also shows new school data, this week's programme activity, and district milestones
Clearer colours — the greeting is now easy to read on any screen
Updates now apply in a single tap

2026.06.18.31.title: Your new home screen
highlights: The app now opens on a personal home feed: your term progress, any follow-up that needs you, and one clear button to record a visit
Your follow-up card links straight into My dashboard — nothing has moved or been removed
More feed cards (new school data, peer activity) arrive in the next update

2026.06.18.30.title: Behind-the-scenes improvements
highlights: Small internal improvements to help us understand and improve the app — no change to how you use it

2026.06.18.29.title: Submission safety
highlights: Tapping Submit twice on a slow connection can no longer create a duplicate record
Small data-quality fix behind the scenes

2026.06.18.28.title: Smoother on your phone
highlights: The screen no longer jumps when you tap options or open dropdowns
Text boxes no longer zoom the page when you tap them
Bigger, easier touch targets throughout
Each step now starts at the top of the screen

2026.06.18.27.title: A simpler Step 4
highlights: Step 4 now shows just the essentials: support needed, support level, and follow-up
Everything else — school context, what is in place, numbers, documents — sits behind one “Add more detail” button
Nothing was removed: every field is still there when you need it

2026.06.18.26.title: One-tap updates
highlights: Tapping Update now applies the new version in a single tap — no more repeat taps
Your emailed School Visit Report no longer includes internal DDD usage fields

2026.06.18.25.title: Dashboard fix
highlights: Fixed: My dashboard would not open after submitting an intervention
Your emailed report now shows subject results in a clear table

2026.06.18.24.title: Cleaner start, clearer wording
highlights: Record new intervention now always starts blank — use the Resume card to continue a saved draft
Step 3 now simply asks what you wanted to know from DDD — six quick questions, tap all that apply
Colleagues you tag now receive their notification reliably
Plain, full wording throughout your emailed School Visit Report

2026.06.18.23.title: One clean emailed School Visit Report
highlights: After you submit, one School Visit Report PDF is emailed to you — school details and performance data filled in automatically
No more duplicate emails
Cleaner report layout with proper page flow

2026.06.18.22.title: Quick school-practices check
highlights: New optional one-tap questions in Step 4: curriculum plans, improvement plan (APIP), and time management
Feeds your emailed School Visit Report — coming next
Skip them entirely if they don't apply to your visit

2026.06.18.21.title: Emailed copies now send reliably
highlights: Your PDF copy is now generated more reliably, even on a shaky connection
Groundwork for automatic emailed visit reports

2026.06.18.20.title: Clearer timing, and groundwork for auto-filled reports
highlights: Per-entry time estimate updated to 6–10 minutes
Each logged visit now links to its school's official record behind the scenes — no action needed
This paves the way for reports that fill in school data automatically

2026.06.18.19.title: Notification wording + sending tweaks
highlights: Clearer wording on the notifications prompt
Admins can now add several people to one notification more easily

2026.06.18.18.title: Turn on phone notifications in one tap
highlights: After signing in, you'll see a quick option to turn on phone notifications
Once on, you can be reminded to log in even when the app is closed
Don't want it? Tap “Not now” — you can always turn it on later in Settings

2026.06.18.17.title: Notifications — behind-the-scenes improvements
highlights: Improvements to how phone notifications are delivered
A small fix so your notification settings link correctly to your profile
No action needed — email and PIN sign-in are unchanged

2026.06.18.16.title: Phone notifications — early access
highlights: You can now turn on phone notifications from the Settings section
Once on, you can be notified to log in even when the app is closed
We're rolling this out gradually — nothing else changes

2026.06.18.15.title: Sign-in update
highlights: We've temporarily removed the biometric (fingerprint/face) sign-in option while we improve it
Email and PIN sign-in is unchanged and works exactly as before
Nothing else changes — you don't need to do anything

2026.06.18.14.title: Sign in with your fingerprint or face
highlights: On devices where you've set up biometric sign-in, a 'Sign in with fingerprint or face' button now appears on the login screen
Tap it to sign in without typing your email and PIN
Email and PIN still works exactly as before, on every device

2026.06.18.13.title: Biometric sign-in — early access
highlights: You can now set up fingerprint or face sign-in from the Settings section
We're rolling this out gradually — email and PIN sign-in stays exactly the same
Find it under Settings, tap ‘Set up’, and follow your phone's prompt

2026.06.18.12.title: Groundwork for biometric sign-in
highlights: Behind-the-scenes preparation for an upcoming option to sign in with your fingerprint or face
Nothing changes for now — you still sign in with your email and PIN as usual

2026.06.18.11.title: You can now reach the version number at the bottom
highlights: On phones, the footer and version button were hidden behind the bottom menu — they now sit clear of it so you can tap the version for details

2026.06.18.10.title: Larger reading text on forms and the review screen
highlights: Helper text, section subtitles, the review screen and similar reading text are now slightly larger and easier to read
Menus, tabs and labels are unchanged so nothing shifts position

2026.06.18.9.title: Green and orange text is easier to read
highlights: Green and orange text (status labels, numbers and headings) is now a darker shade so it reads clearly on white
Coloured buttons, borders and highlights keep their familiar bright colours

2026.06.18.8.title: The bottom menu now stays put everywhere
highlights: Record, Dashboard and Toolkit now stay at the bottom of the screen at all times — including while you fill in an intervention
No more jumping to the top: the menu is always in the same place and always within thumb's reach
While recording, the Next/Back buttons sit just above the menu; leaving a half-finished intervention still saves a draft

2026.06.18.7.title: Clearer text on the green action buttons
highlights: The label on the green “Record new intervention” button (and the update button) is now dark instead of white, making it much easier to read
The buttons keep their familiar green colour

2026.06.18.6.title: Get back to My Dashboard while recording an intervention
highlights: While filling in an intervention on a phone, you can now jump to My Dashboard or the Toolkit from the buttons at the top — no need to sign out
If you leave a half-finished intervention, it's saved as a draft automatically so you can pick up where you left off
Nothing changes on desktop

2026.06.18.5.title: Confirmation messages stay long enough to read
highlights: Pop-up messages now stay on screen longer, and longer messages stay up longer still
Tap a message to dismiss it once you've read it
Messages are now read aloud by screen readers for users who rely on them

2026.06.18.4.title: Larger text in more places
highlights: The smallest captions and labels (like comment times, summaries and filter labels) are now larger and easier to read
Nothing moves or changes — the same screens, with more readable text

2026.06.18.3.title: Clearer, easier-to-read text
highlights: Smaller grey text (hints, labels and captions) is now darker and easier to read
The labels on the bottom navigation bar are clearer too
Nothing changes in how the app works — the same screens, just more comfortable to read

2026.06.18.2.title: Your phone now gives a gentle buzz to confirm a save
highlights: When you submit a visit, your phone gives a soft confirming buzz — including when it's saved offline to send later
If a required field is still missing, you'll feel a firmer buzz so a blocked Submit is hard to miss
Works on Android phones; iPhones and phones with vibration switched off simply feel nothing, and everything else works exactly the same

2026.06.18.1.title: Next and Back buttons stay in reach while you fill in a visit
highlights: On phones, the Next and Back buttons now stay fixed at the bottom of the screen while you work through a visit
No more scrolling to the end of a step to move forward or back — they're always within thumb's reach
Nothing changes on desktop, and your information is unaffected

2026.06.13.4.title: Fix for the bottom bar Dashboard button
highlights: Fixed the Dashboard button on the new bottom bar, which wasn't responding to taps
Pop-up confirmation messages now sit clear of the bottom bar

2026.06.13.3.title: New bottom navigation bar on phones
highlights: On phones, there's now a fixed bar at the bottom with Record, Dashboard, and Toolkit
Tap to jump between the main sections without scrolling back to the top
The bar tucks away while you're filling in a visit, so it stays out of your way

2026.06.13.2.title: Bigger, bolder home screen buttons
highlights: The main home screen buttons (Record, Dashboard, Toolkit) are now larger, card-style buttons
They have a soft shadow and a subtle press animation, so they feel more responsive when tapped
Easier to find and tap, especially on a phone

2026.06.13.1.title: Easier tapping on your phone
highlights: Tag chips, toolkit tabs, and time-period buttons are now bigger and easier to tap
Fewer accidental and missed taps when logging a visit on a phone
Nothing changes in how the app works — just larger, more comfortable touch targets

2026.05.25.3.title: Location capture on submissions
highlights: Submissions now capture your phone GPS location as evidence of the visit
Location appears on the audit PDF with a Google Maps link reviewers can click
Location capture happens automatically when you reach the review step — no extra taps
If location is unavailable or you decline, your submission still goes through normally
You'll see a confirmation card showing your captured location before you submit

2026.05.25.2.title: Security & reliability fix
highlights: Fixed an issue where the app could occasionally display the wrong official's profile after a long period of inactivity
Improved session handling so your account information is always loaded correctly
If you've been using the app today, please sign out and sign in again to refresh your session

2026.05.25.1.title: Now installable as an app!
highlights: The Tracker is now installable as a mobile app on Android (home screen) and as a desktop app on Windows/Mac
Faster loading and works offline for the app shell
New version notifications when updates are published
Version information is now visible in the footer
